plants.name: Rice
plants.description: A staple cereal crop that grows well in fertile river plains.
plants.plantingTips: Keep soil consistently moist during early growth; transplant seedlings after 20–25 days.

plants.name: Wheat
plants.description: A cool-season cereal crop commonly grown on fertile plains.
plants.plantingTips: Sow in well-prepared soil; irrigate at crown-root initiation and flowering stages.

plants.name: Sugarcane
plants.description: A high-biomass crop that prefers deep, fertile soils.
plants.plantingTips: Plant setts in furrows; maintain regular irrigation and apply organic manure.

plants.name: Maize
plants.description: A versatile cereal that performs well in fertile, well-drained soils.
plants.plantingTips: Sow at proper spacing; avoid waterlogging and provide balanced NPK fertilization.

plants.name: Pulses (Lentil/Chickpea)
plants.description: Protein-rich legumes that fit well in crop rotations on plains.
plants.plantingTips: Sow in cooler months; inoculate seed with Rhizobium for better nodulation.

plants.name: Pearl Millet (Bajra)
plants.description: A hardy cereal suited to low-rainfall regions.
plants.plantingTips: Sow with the first rains; use wider spacing and conserve soil moisture.

plants.name: Cluster Bean (Guar)
plants.description: A drought-tolerant legume crop grown in dry areas.
plants.plantingTips: Sow at onset of monsoon; avoid excess irrigation and ensure good drainage.

plants.name: Barley
plants.description: A resilient cereal that can tolerate relatively poor moisture conditions.
plants.plantingTips: Sow in cool season; irrigate lightly if needed and avoid waterlogging.

plants.name: Moth Bean
plants.description: A drought-resistant pulse suitable for arid climates.
plants.plantingTips: Sow after first effective rains; use shallow sowing and minimum tillage where possible.

plants.name: Sesame
plants.description: An oilseed crop that can perform under low rainfall with good drainage.
plants.plantingTips: Avoid waterlogging; sow on ridges in heavier soils and keep field weed-free early.

plants.name: Cotton
plants.description: A major fiber crop well known for thriving in moisture-retentive soils.
plants.plantingTips: Use proper spacing for airflow; manage pests and avoid prolonged standing water.

plants.name: Soybean
plants.description: An important oilseed and protein crop for fertile, moisture-holding soils.
plants.plantingTips: Sow with monsoon onset; ensure good seed inoculation and timely weeding.

plants.name: Sorghum (Jowar)
plants.description: A drought-tolerant cereal often grown in heavy soils.
plants.plantingTips: Sow after first rains; maintain plant population and manage shoot fly early.

plants.name: Pigeon Pea (Tur)
plants.description: A deep-rooted pulse crop that fits well in rainfed farming.
plants.plantingTips: Sow at wider spacing; avoid waterlogging and provide staking support if needed.

plants.name: Groundnut (Peanut)
plants.description: An oilseed crop that can be grown in well-drained black soils with good management.
plants.plantingTips: Ensure loose topsoil for pod development; avoid excess nitrogen and keep weeds low.

plants.name: Cashew
plants.description: A hardy tree crop commonly grown in laterite-dominated coastal regions.
plants.plantingTips: Plant on slopes with drainage; mulch to conserve moisture during dry periods.

plants.name: Tea
plants.description: A plantation crop typically grown in humid hill tracts and certain laterite regions.
plants.plantingTips: Maintain acidic, well-drained soil; prune regularly and mulch with organic matter.

plants.name: Coffee
plants.description: A plantation crop that prefers well-drained soils and partial shade.
plants.plantingTips: Grow under shade trees; maintain mulching and avoid standing water near roots.

plants.name: Rubber
plants.description: A plantation crop grown in warm, humid climates with good drainage.
plants.plantingTips: Plant in pits enriched with compost; keep the base weed-free and mulch well.

plants.name: Coconut
plants.description: A coastal plantation crop that can grow in well-drained lateritic soils.
plants.plantingTips: Provide regular watering in dry months; apply organic manure and ensure drainage.

plants.name: Apple
plants.description: A temperate fruit crop grown in cooler hill regions.
plants.plantingTips: Plant on slopes with good drainage; prune annually and protect from late frost.

plants.name: Potato
plants.description: A cool-season tuber crop suitable for well-drained hill soils.
plants.plantingTips: Plant seed tubers in ridges; keep soil loose and hill up to protect tubers from light.

plants.name: Peas
plants.description: A cool-season legume that grows well in many hill tracts.
plants.plantingTips: Sow in cool weather; provide light support for climbing varieties.

plants.name: Cabbage
plants.description: A cool-season vegetable often cultivated in hill regions.
plants.plantingTips: Transplant healthy seedlings; keep consistent moisture and watch for caterpillars.

plants.name: Strawberry
plants.description: A fruit crop suitable for cooler climates with good organic matter.
plants.plantingTips: Use mulch to reduce weeds; keep soil evenly moist and avoid wetting flowers too much.

plants.name: Millets (Ragi/Jowar)
plants.description: Hardy cereals commonly grown in rainfed regions with lighter soils.
plants.plantingTips: Sow with rainfall onset; keep weeds controlled in early growth stages.

plants.name: Pulses (Tur/Gram)
plants.description: Legume crops often suited to red soils under rainfed farming.
plants.plantingTips: Sow at correct spacing; manage weeds and apply phosphorus as needed.

plants.name: Groundnut (Peanut)
plants.description: An oilseed crop frequently grown in red soils with good drainage.
plants.plantingTips: Ensure loose topsoil for peg penetration; avoid prolonged moisture stress during flowering.

plants.name: Cotton
plants.description: A fiber crop grown in several soil types with proper nutrient management.
plants.plantingTips: Maintain spacing and pest monitoring; avoid waterlogging and support with balanced fertilization.

plants.name: Tobacco
plants.description: A commercial crop grown in certain red soil belts.
plants.plantingTips: Transplant on raised beds; manage irrigation carefully and avoid excess nitrogen.

plants.name: Maize
plants.description: A cereal crop grown widely across climates where drainage is good.
plants.plantingTips: Sow at recommended spacing; apply balanced nutrients and avoid waterlogging.

plants.name: Groundnut (Peanut)
plants.description: An oilseed crop that grows well where soils are well-drained.
plants.plantingTips: Provide calcium/gypsum at pegging; keep field weed-free early for best yields.

plants.name: Cotton
plants.description: A fiber crop that can be grown with good nutrient and water management.
plants.plantingTips: Keep soil moisture steady; monitor pests and avoid excessive nitrogen late season.

plants.name: Rice
plants.description: In suitable climates, rice can be cultivated with adequate water and field preparation.
plants.plantingTips: Use puddling and maintain standing water during early stages; drain before harvest.

plants.name: Banana
plants.description: A fruit crop that prefers fertile soils with consistent moisture.
plants.plantingTips: Provide regular irrigation; use mulch and wind protection for healthy bunch development.
